data.title: Payrolls Rather Than Fed Meeting Could Be Catalyst for 10-Year Treasury Yields — Market Talk
data.description: The Federal Reserve's meeting is unlikely to be the catalyst to break the 10-year Treasury yield from its recent tight trading range--even oil prices are no longer moving the needle, says Nuveen's Laura Cooper in a note. "Next week's payrolls could be the pivot," the global investment strategist says. Ongoing upbeat data points to policy being less restrictive than assumed, raising the bar for interest-rate cuts relative to market pricing, says Cooper, who is also head of macro credit. "Yet as inflationary effects ease, there is scope for a late-2026 cut to be priced back in," she says. The 10-year Treasury yield last trades at 4.347%, down 0.7 bps, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Sweden's Swedbank Q1 profit falls 10% as interest income slips
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden-based retail bank's Q1 profit fell 10% yr/yr as total income declined

  • Net interest income dropped 3% yr/yr, while net commission income rose 3%

  • Company cites stable results amid uncertainty and ongoing reorganizations for growth

Outlook

  • Swedbank did not provide specific financial guidance for upcoming quarters or the full year

Result Drivers

  • UNCERTAIN ENVIRONMENT - Co said results were stable despite external uncertainty

  • REORGANISATIONS - Co said it is reorganizing for stronger customer focus and growing volumes

  • STRUCTURAL IMPROVEMENTS - Co said structural improvements are the next step for growth and efficiency

data.title: Deutsche Bank posts bigger-than-expected profit in Q1 despite risks
data.description:
Deutsche Bank on Wednesday posted a bigger-than expected first-quarter profit despite higher credit risk provisions and negative currency impacts.

The bank also upgraded its outlook for revenue at the investment bank for 2026, expecting revenue now to be higher rather than just slightly higher.

Deutsche, Germany's largest lender, recorded net profit attributable to shareholders of 1.9 billion euros ($2.22 billion) in the quarter, up from a profit of 1.8 billion euros a year earlier. It is better than analyst expectations for a profit of 1.768 billion euros.

The bank recorded provisions for credit losses of 519 million euros, up from 471 million euros a year ago and higher than analyst expectations for 447 million euros.

The bank said provisions include an overlay "reflecting macroeconomic uncertainties".

($1 = 0.8544 euros)

data.title: Fed's Policy Seen in Good Place — Market Talk
data.description: The Federal Reserve's patience this week will be a prudent approach, with policy in a good place, Nuveen's Laura Cooper says in a note. March data has shown resilience, from solid payrolls to contained inflation and upbeat retail sales, suggesting U.S. consumers have a cushion to absorb the energy shock, the global investment strategist says. "Financial conditions remain supportive, leaving the Fed's data-dependent messaging likely unchanged," says Cooper, who is also head of macro credit. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Coal India Set to Benefit From Long-Term Tailwinds — Market Talk
data.description: Coal India stands to benefit from long-term tailwinds, Elara Securities (India) analysts say in a research report. These include rising acceptance of coal as a dominant fuel mix, strong delivery volumes in the past few quarters and better evacuation infrastructure in terms of first-mile connectivity projects, the analysts say. The company's coal production is likely to pick up in FY 2027 thanks to rising power demand in India, the analysts say. Peak demand reached a record high 256 gigawatts in April, and is expected to hit 270GW in FY 2027. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to 522.00 rupees from 458.00 rupees with an unchanged accumulate rating. Shares are 2.2% higher at 477.05 rupees. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Straumann first quarter sales beat expectations
data.description:
Swiss dental implants maker Straumann on Wednesday reported first quarter sales above expectations, citing strong momentum in implant sales.

Revenue declined to 673 million Swiss francs ($853 million) in the first quarter, down 1.2% but up 7.1% organically, beating analysts' estimate of 665.5 million francs in a Vara consensus.

The company confirmed its 2026 guidance, expecting high single digit organic revenue growth and an earnings margin improvement of 30 to 60 basis points.

($1 = 0.7889 Swiss francs)

data.title: Aspocomp Q1 Order Book Record High, Net Sales Decreased
Belgium's Ontex Q1 revenue falls 4% on weak baby care demand
data.description:
Overview

  • Belgium hygiene products maker's Q1 revenue fell 4% like-for-like, driven by lower baby care demand

  • Adjusted EBITDA margin dropped 2.2pp yr/yr to 9.1%

  • Company reduced net debt by 5% over the quarter to €550 mln

Outlook

  • Ontex maintains 2026 outlook, expecting adjusted EBITDA to increase by around 10%

  • Company expects free cash flow to turn positive in 2026

  • Ontex expects leverage ratio to decrease to 3x or lower by year end

Result Drivers

  • BABY CARE DECLINE - Company said lower baby care volumes, especially in Europe and retailer brands, drove revenue decrease

  • ADULT CARE GROWTH - Adult care volumes rose 2%, supported by mid-single-digit retail channel growth and stable healthcare demand in Europe

  • HIGHER COSTS - Increased costs for backsheets, packaging, transportation and supply chain inefficiencies weighed on profitability, partly offset by cost transformation program

data.title: Germany's Mercedes-Benz Q1 revenue falls 4.9% on China competition
data.description:
Overview

  • Germany luxury automaker's Q1 group revenue fell 4.9% yr/yr amid challenging market conditions

  • Adjusted EBIT for Mercedes-Benz Cars declined 47.2% yr/yr to €933 mln

  • Company cites intense competition in China and model changeovers as factors behind lower results

Outlook

  • Mercedes-Benz confirms full-year Group revenue at prior-year level and EBIT significantly above prior year

  • Company expects free cash flow of industrial business slightly below 2025 level

  • Mercedes-Benz continues to monitor geopolitical risks, including Middle East conflict, for impact on consumer confidence

Result Drivers

  • CHINA MARKET PRESSURE - Intense competition and subdued demand in China weighed on car sales and profitability

  • EUROPE AND U.S. GROWTH - Sales growth in Europe (+7%) and the U.S. (+20%) partly offset declines in China

  • BEV SALES GROWTH EUROPE - Battery-electric vehicle sales in Europe rose 34%, with BEV order intake more than doubling year-on-year

Key Details

Metric

Q1 Adjusted EBIT

Beat/Miss

Actual

EUR 933 mln

Consensus Estimate

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy", 12 "hold" and 6 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the auto & truck manufacturers peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Mercedes-Benz Group AG is €60.00, about 22.4% above its April 28 closing price of €49.03

  • The stock recently traded at 8 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 9 three months ago

data.title: Siltronic posts first quarter sales below expectations
data.description:
German semiconductor materials supplier Siltronic reported lower-than-expected quarterly sales on Wednesday, due to a challenging market environment.

The company, which makes silicon wafers used in semiconductor chips, posted first-quarter revenue of 306.5 million euros ($359 million), down from 345.8 million euros a year earlier, and below analysts' average forecast of 317 million euros, according to a poll by LSEG.

($1 = 0.8544 euros)

data.title: Mercedes reports narrower-than-forecast profit slump in first quarter
data.description:
Mercedes-Benz on Wednesday reported a slump in first-quarter operating profit but beat market expectations, as the German premium carmaker battles to shore up dwindling margins and revive weak demand in key markets like China.

The automaker reported first-quarter earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) of 1.9 billion euros ($2.22 billion), down 17% year on year but higher than the average analyst estimate of 1.6 billion euros, according to a poll conducted by Visible Alpha.

Steep tariffs, China woes and a rocky transition to electric vehicles have weighed heavily on German carmakers like Mercedes, whose CEO Ola Kaellenius has turned to sweeping redundancies and cost cuts to stem losses while rolling out a cascade of new models.

The Stuttgart-based company posted first-quarter revenue of 31.6 billion euros, missing analyst estimates for 31.8 billion euros.

The adjusted return on sales for Mercedes' core cars division was 4.1%, within a full-year target of between 3 and 5%, but down from 7.3% in the same quarter last year.

($1 = 0.8543 euros)
